title: Andhra Punugulu (Idli Batter Fritters)

subs.name: 2 cups rava (semolina) mixed with curd to a thick batter; ferment 30 minutes

ingredients.name: rice flour
visual_checks: helps the fritters hold shape and adds crispness
ingredients.notes: helps the fritters hold shape and adds crispness

subs.name: air-fry at 200 °C for 12 minutes, turning once, for a lower-oil version

steps.text: Combine the idli batter with chopped onion, green chillies, ginger, cumin seeds, rice flour and salt. Mix well — the batter should be thick enough to hold a ball shape when scooped with a spoon. If it spreads, add another tablespoon of rice flour.
Heat oil in a kadai to 175 °C (a drop of batter should sink slightly, then rise and sizzle briskly). Use a medium-depth oil — at least 6 cm.
Using a wet spoon or your fingers, gently drop tablespoon-sized balls of batter into the hot oil. Fry 6–8 at a time without crowding. Turn them after 2 minutes so they colour evenly.
Continue frying for another 2–3 minutes until the punugulu are deep golden and crisp all over. They should sound hollow when tapped with a spoon. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper.
Serve hot with coconut chutney and red onion tomato chutney. Punugulu lose crispness within 20 minutes, so eat them straight from the oil.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.

substitution_notes: idli-dosa-batter → 2 cups rava (semolina) mixed with curd to a thick batter; ferment 30 minutes; oil → air-fry at 200 °C for 12 minutes, turning once, for a lower-oil version
